summary: Mobile UX audit at 390px for /ground-0-briefing and /portal (authenticated paid session). Ground0 page passes all checks. Authenticated portal has one critical horizontal overflow bug in the portal header strip.

ui_bugs.issue: Horizontal overflow on mobile 390px. The dark header bar (.#000F1F) contains a nowrap flex row with 'LAUNCHPATH COHORT PORTAL', dash, 'SYSTEM_STATUS_MONITOR | LPOS v1.0', OPERATOR name, and Sign out button — all in one flex row with no wrap and no mobile media query. At 390px: body.scrollWidth=480, windowInnerWidth=390. Users can horizontally scroll the page. OPERATOR name and Sign out button are clipped/cut off to the right.
G0-1, G0-4 module code labels wrap to 2 lines in accordion ('G0-\n1') on 390px due to minWidth:28 constraint combined with 1.5rem gap and title text. Minor readability issue.

issues: SYSTEM_STATUS_MONITOR bar overflows horizontally at 390px. OPERATOR info is clipped. No mobile-specific hiding/wrapping of this strip is implemented. Causes body horizontal scroll.

action_items: Fix portal header strip overflow on mobile: Add media query (max-width: 768px) to hide the OPERATOR name and Sign out button from the SYSTEM_STATUS_MONITOR strip, OR add overflow:hidden to the strip container, OR add flex-wrap:wrap with proper stacking. The strip inner div (maxWidth:1200, display:flex, gap:1rem) needs a mobile breakpoint.
Optional: Fix Ground0 accordion module code labels wrapping (G0-1 etc) — increase minWidth from 28px or use white-space:nowrap on the code span
critical_code_review_comments: PortalPage.jsx line 324: portal header strip inner div (maxWidth:1200, display:flex, alignItems:center, gap:1rem) has no flexWrap and no mobile breakpoint. The @media (max-width: 768px) block in the style tag only handles .portal-layout and .portal-sidebar/.portal-main, not the header strip. Add a rule to hide or collapse the user-info section on mobile.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 37 — mobile audit of /ground-0-briefing and /portal at 390px. Ground0 passes all checks: hamburger PASS, hero heading PASS, email form full-width 62px PASS, submit button full-width 60px PASS, accordion expand/collapse PASS, sin wages grid collapses to 1col PASS, no horizontal overflow PASS. Portal unauthenticated screen: hamburger PASS, Google login button 60px full-width PASS, no overflow PASS. Portal authenticated (sess_paid_1773353168): sidebar stacks vertically (column flex) PASS, SignalMonitor 350px width no overflow PASS, 10 task items rendered, sign out button 54px PASS — BUT portal header strip (SYSTEM_STATUS_MONITOR bar) causes body.scrollWidth=480 (90px overflow). Fix needed in PortalPage.jsx style tag media query.

mobile_test_results.ground0_email_form: PASS - input 342px wide, 62px height; button 342px wide, 60px height
mobile_test_results.portal_auth_signal_monitor: PASS - 350px wide, circular gauge renders correctly
mobile_test_results.portal_auth_horizontal_overflow: FAIL - body.scrollWidth=480, caused by SYSTEM_STATUS_MONITOR header strip

rca_of_issue.issue: Portal authenticated view horizontal overflow at 390px
rca_of_issue.root_cause: PortalPage.jsx: The portal header bar (background #000F1F) contains an inner div with maxWidth:1200, display:flex, gap:1rem, alignItems:center — no flexWrap, no overflow:hidden. It holds 4 items: 'LAUNCHPATH COHORT PORTAL' label + dash separator + 'SYSTEM_STATUS_MONITOR | LPOS v1.0' (flex:1) + user info div (OPERATOR name + Sign out button). At 390px, the combined natural width is ~480px. The existing @media (max-width: 768px) block only covers .portal-layout and .portal-sidebar.
rca_of_issue.fix: Add to PortalPage.jsx style tag: @media (max-width: 768px) { .portal-header-userinfo { display: none !important; } } — and add className='portal-header-userinfo' to the user info div. OR add overflow:hidden to the strip container.
rca_of_issue.severity: HIGH - users can horizontally scroll the page, OPERATOR info visually clipped
